MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-21-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS NANCY ASCHER AND THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE MET THEIR INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION BY SUBMITTING THE DECLARATION OF ROBERT MONTGOMERY, M.D., WHO OPINES THAT NANCY ASCHER, M.D., AND THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA MET THE STANDARD OF CARE IN THEIR CARE AND TREATMENT OF PLAINTIFF. "WHEN A DEFENDANT MOVES FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND SUPPORTS HIS MOTION WITH EXPERT DECLARATIONS THAT HIS CONDUCT FELL WITHIN THE COMMUNITY STANDARD OF CARE, HE IS ENTITLED TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT UNLESS THE PLAINTIFF COMES FORWARD WITH CONFLICTING EXPERT EVIDENCE." (MUNRO V. REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA (1989) 215 CAL.APP.3D 977, 985.) DR. MONTGOMERY'S DECLARATION PROVIDES A SUFFICIENT EXPLANATION FOR HIS CONCLUSION THAT DEFENDANTS MET THE STANDARD OF CARE AND THE DECLARATION MEETS THE FOUNDATIONAL REQUIREMENT FOR ADMISSIBILITY. BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SUBMITTED CONFLICTING EXPERT EVIDENCE IN RESPONSE TO DEFENDANTS' MOTION, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE DEFENDANTS MET THE RELEVANT STANDARD OF CARE. THEREFORE, DEFENDANTS ARE ENTITLED TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOSEPH QUINN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-22-2016 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Kinnie telephone number 510-776-6154 Plaintiff in Propria Persona.
No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court noted that Mr. Kinnie did not provide notice to the opposing party of his intent to argue after posting of the tentative ruling. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS NANCY ASCHER AND THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is granted. Defendants have met their initial burden of production by submitting the declaration of Robert Montgomery, M.D., who opines that Nancy Ascher, M.D., and the Regents of the University of California met the standard of care in their care and treatment of Plaintiff. "When a defendant moves for summary judgment and supports his motion with expert declarations that his conduct fell within the community standard of care, he is entitled to summary judgment unless the plaintiff comes forward with conflicting expert evidence." (Munro v. Regents of University of California (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 977, 985.) Dr. Montgomery's declaration provides a sufficient explanation for his conclusion that Defendants met the standard of care and the declaration meets the foundational requirement for admissibility. Because Plaintiff has not submitted conflicting expert evidence in response to Defendants' motion, Plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether the Defendants met the relevant standard of care. Therefore, Defendants are entitled to summary judgment. Order and judgment signed in open Court.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)
